body:0002.source: Allows platform administrators to generate, customize, and share reports with platform/project data.
body:0002.target: Allows platform administrators to generate, customize, and share reports with platform/project data.

body:0003.source: Provides clear, visual, tailored reports for colleagues or community
body:0003.target: Provides clear, visual, tailored reports for colleagues or community

body:0004.source: Pulls data from ideation projects, surveys, and visitor insights
body:0004.target: Pulls data from ideation projects, surveys, and visitor insights

body:0005.source: Supports both PDF sharing and mobile-friendly phase reports
body:0005.target: Supports both PDF sharing and mobile-friendly phase reports

body:0007.source: You can find reports in the Reporting tab in the back office, these are what we call platform reports. You can also create reports directly from an information phase, which we refer to as phase reports.
body:0007.target: You can find reports in the Reporting tab in the back office, these are what we call platform reports. You can also create reports directly from an information phase, which we refer to as phase reports.

body:0008.source: ⚠️ Platform reports and project reports are separate. Platform reports can't be added to a project, and project reports can't appear in the Reporting tab.
body:0008.target: ⚠️ Platform reports and project reports are separate. Platform reports can't be added to a project, and project reports can't appear in the Reporting tab.

body:0010.source: Platform reports are available to admins only and provide an overview of platform-wide data, including:
body:0010.target: Platform reports are available to admins only and provide an overview of platform-wide data, including:

body:0014.source: By default, Community Monitor reports are included here. To learn more about the Community Monitor feature, see our article:
body:0014.target: By default, Community Monitor reports are included here. To learn more about the Community Monitor feature, see our article:

body:0017.source: Project reports are created within a specific project, during a Share Information phase.
body:0017.target: Project reports are created within a specific project, during a Share Information phase.

body:0018.source: They can be made visible in the front office, allowing you to share results directly with users.
body:0018.target: They can be made visible in the front office, allowing you to share results directly with users.

body:0019.source: Different widgets in the report builder
body:0019.target: Different widgets in the report builder

body:0020.source: Widgets are reusable blocks you drag into your report.
body:0020.target: Widgets are reusable blocks you drag into your report.

body:0022.source: Text: gives you a box where you can freely write and edit some text
body:0022.target: Text: gives you a box where you can freely write and edit some text

body:0023.source: Image: gives you the ability to add an image to the report
body:0023.target: Image: gives you the ability to add an image to the report

body:0024.source: 2 column: gives you the ability to create 2 columns in your report
body:0024.target: 2 column: gives you the ability to create 2 columns in your report

body:0025.source: White space: gives you the ability to add a blank section within your report
body:0025.target: White space: gives you the ability to add a blank section within your report

body:0026.source: Embed: gives you the ability to embed for example a video in your report
body:0026.target: Embed: gives you the ability to embed for example a video in your report

body:0028.source: Survey Questions: you can visualize specific survey results in your report
body:0028.target: Survey Questions: you can visualize specific survey results in your report

body:0029.source: Most reacted inputs: you can show the most reacted inputs in your report
body:0029.target: Most reacted inputs: you can show the most reacted inputs in your report

body:0030.source: Input: you can show a specific (manually selected) input in your report
body:0030.target: Input: you can show a specific (manually selected) input in your report

body:0031.source: Community Monitor Health Score: you can show the health score of your community monitor
body:0031.target: Community Monitor Health Score: you can show the health score of your community monitor

body:0032.source: Visitor timeline: you show the visitors timeline in your report. It also show number of visitors, number of visits, visit duration and number of page view per visit.
body:0032.target: Visitor timeline: you show the visitors timeline in your report. It also show number of visitors, number of visits, visit duration and number of page view per visit.

body:0033.source: Participants timeline: you show the participants timeline in your report
body:0033.target: Participants timeline: you show the participants timeline in your report

body:0034.source: Registration timeline: you show the registration timeline in your report
body:0034.target: Registration timeline: you show the registration timeline in your report

body:0035.source: Traffic sources: you can show where users are coming from in your report
body:0035.target: Traffic sources: you can show where users are coming from in your report

body:0036.source: Demographics: you can show certain demographics in your report
body:0036.target: Demographics: you can show certain demographics in your report

body:0037.source: Participation: you can show participation in your report
body:0037.target: Participation: you can show participation in your report

body:0038.source: Methods used: you can show which methods are used in your report
body:0038.target: Methods used: you can show which methods are used in your report

body:0039.source: Projects: you can show which projects are on the platform in your report
body:0039.target: Projects: you can show which projects are on the platform in your report

body:0040.source: How to create and customize a report?
body:0040.target: How to create and customize a report?

body:0044.source: Project-level: create Share information phase > click Report > Create a report. Watch video below:
body:0044.target: Project-level: create Share information phase > click Report > Create a report. Watch video below:

body:0049.source: Add Report Title and start building
body:0049.target: Add Report Title and start building

body:0051.source: Use the drag-and-drop interface for fast setup
body:0051.target: Use the drag-and-drop interface for fast setup

body:0053.source: Right-hand panel: Editor for modifying selected elements
body:0053.target: Right-hand panel: Editor for modifying selected elements

body:0055.source: Use data grouping to break down survey results by different segments, helping you uncover patterns and insights across different groups of participants.
body:0055.target: Use data grouping to break down survey results by different segments, helping you uncover patterns and insights across different groups of participants.

body:0056.source: You can group your survey data by:
body:0056.target: You can group your survey data by:

body:0058.source: Survey answers (group responses to one question by how people answered another question)
body:0058.target: Survey answers (group responses to one question by how people answered another question)

body:0059.source: You can add grouping in your report by:
body:0059.target: You can add grouping in your report by:

body:0060.source: Step 1: Adding a survey question widget (see image below)
body:0060.target: Step 1: Adding a survey question widget (see image below)

body:0061.source: Step 2: Select a question in your survey
body:0061.target: Step 2: Select a question in your survey

body:0062.source: Step 3: Select what type of grouping you would like to apply
body:0062.target: Step 3: Select what type of grouping you would like to apply

body:0063.source: Step 4: Select the specific attribute that you want to use to group the data
body:0063.target: Step 4: Select the specific attribute that you want to use to group the data

body:0064.source: This lets you answer questions like:
body:0064.target: This lets you answer questions like:

body:0065.source: “Do younger participants rate our service differently than older ones?”
body:0065.target: “Do younger participants rate our service differently than older ones?”

body:0066.source: “How do people from different cities respond to our key questions?”
body:0066.target: “How do people from different cities respond to our key questions?”

body:0067.source: “Does answering ‘Yes’ to Question 3 correlate with higher satisfaction scores?”
body:0067.target: “Does answering ‘Yes’ to Question 3 correlate with higher satisfaction scores?”

body:0068.source: 💡 Grouping helps you find trends and correlations that might be hidden in the overall data.
body:0068.target: 💡 Grouping helps you find trends and correlations that might be hidden in the overall data.

body:0069.source: ℹ️ Access to this feature depends on your plan.
body:0069.target: ℹ️ Access to this feature depends on your plan.

body:0070.source: How to add AI in your report?
body:0070.target: How to add AI in your report?

body:0071.source: AI tab summarizes open text survey questions or phase inputs
body:0071.target: AI tab summarizes open text survey questions or phase inputs

body:0072.source: Select project + phase → AI summary appears → drag summary into report
body:0072.target: Select project + phase → AI summary appears → drag summary into report

body:0074.source: References shown in AI summary don’t carry over to the final report block.
body:0074.target: References shown in AI summary don’t carry over to the final report block.

body:0075.source: Auto tags created in the AI analysis can’t be carried over to the report builder automatically
body:0075.target: Auto tags created in the AI analysis can’t be carried over to the report builder automatically

body:0076.source: How to export and share your report?
body:0076.target: How to export and share your report?

body:0077.source: Attach PDF to a Share Information phase.
body:0077.target: Attach PDF to a Share Information phase.

body:0078.source: Go to a Share information phase > Report > Toggle Visible
body:0078.target: Go to a Share information phase > Report > Toggle Visible

body:0079.source: ⚠️: Platform reports can not be carried over to an information phase
body:0079.target: ⚠️: Platform reports can not be carried over to an information phase

body:0082.source: Download as PDF via: Print > Save as PDF
body:0082.target: Download as PDF via: Print > Save as PDF

body:0083.source: Download as Word via Download > Download as Word
body:0083.target: Download as Word via Download > Download as Word

body:0085.source: Contact support via the the chat bubble on the bottom right corner of your platform if:
body:0085.target: Contact support via the the chat bubble on the bottom right corner of your platform if:

body:0086.source: The Weglot translation button is appearing on your reports
body:0086.target: The Weglot translation button is appearing on your reports

body:0087.source: To get names and emails for voting data as it is not automatically included
body:0087.target: To get names and emails for voting data as it is not automatically included
